edus.text: Hey, i was looking for information to replace a title certificate using mail.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you sending any items to the DMV?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, i'll do it.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Perfect. Then make sure you send a completed form MV-902 also know as Application for Duplicate Title.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Ok. And what about if i apply for a title certificate, instead?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: For that scenario, You'll need to complete form MV-82TON or Application for Title Only. Make sure you read instructions carefully and follow them with accuracy.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Have you moved recently, by the way?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yeah, it has been couple moths already since i moved.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: If that so, then you must learn how to change your address.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I'll get into it, thanks. Now, when i apply for a replacement title, it'll be issued in the DMV office?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: The same day i apply for it, i mean?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Sorry, but that would be a no. For practical reasons, all certificates are printed in a secure facility foreign to the DMV and then mailed to you. That way is more fast and easy.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Um, sounds logical. In any case, what items i need to bring to DMV when i look for the replacement process?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You have to bring with you a completed Application for Duplicate Title or MV-902.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: for the Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33)
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: The Post-9/11 GI Bill Chapter 33 helps you pay for school or job training .
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Have you served on active duty after September 10, 2001?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: yes
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: may qualify for Chapter 33 of GI Bill Post-9/11. Find out if you can get this educational benefit
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: which are the requirements?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Served at least 90 days on active duty either all at once or with breaks in service on or after September 11 , 2001
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: not
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Received a Purple Heart on or after September 11 , 2001 , and were honorably discharged after any amount of service
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: yes
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You can get these educational benefits meet one of the requirements
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I qualify for other VA education benefits too?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You will have to choose which benefit you would like to use. This is an irrevocable decision, which means that you cannot change your mind.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hello, I forgot o update my address, can you help me with that?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: hi, you have to report any change of address to DMV within 10 days after moving. You should do this both for the address associated with your license and all the addresses associated with all your vehicles.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can I do my DMV transactions online?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, you can sign up for MyDMV for all the online transactions needed.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Thanks, and in case I forget to bring all of the documentation needed to the DMV office, what can I do?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: This happens often with our customers so that's why our website and MyDMV are so useful for our customers. Just check if you can make your transaction online so you don't have to go to the DMV Office.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Ok, and can you tell me again where should I report my new address?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Sure. Any change of address must be reported to the DMV, that's for the address associated with your license and any of your vehicles.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can you tell me more about Traffic points and their cost?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Traffic points is the system used by DMV to track dangerous drivers. The cost of the traffic points is independent of the DRA, so you get a separate charge based on the total points you accumulate.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Should I apply for a scholarship? Do you have any advice about it?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, it's a good idea to apply for as many scholarships as you can since is free money for college or career school. You should start researching as soon as possible and meet deadlines so you can have higher chances of scholarship success.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Would a scholarship affect my other student aid?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, it would because all of your student aid added together can't be more than your cost of attendance at your college or career school.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Do you have any more questions about this?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I think that's enough for now, thanks.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Now, can you tell me how to get my scholarship money?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: It depends on the scholarship. The money may be sent directly to your college, where it will be applied to any tuition, fees, or other amounts you owe and then any leftover funds are given to you. Or it might be sent directly to you in a check.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Have the scholarship provider informed you what to expect when they awarded you the scholarship?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: The didn't, I'll make sure to ask them. Thanks.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: I am having a few problems understanding the financial aid that is available through the FSA, could you help me with it please?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you currently preparing for college or a career school?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, I am
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We have a dedicated piece on our website that informs you how financial aid works, gives you insights into what types of financial aid are available and also helps you to calculate what amount of financial aid you may be eligible for
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What are the different types of aid that are available through the FSA?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Grants are aids that are available, based on need. These typically don't have to be repaid
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can you give me further information on the FAFSA4caster service?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: This service is used to estimate what aid you may be eligible for and is specific for each and every applicant
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can you explain to me what Work-Study Jobs are in relation to the types of aid that are available?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Work-Study Jobs are a way for students to earn money to help pay for school
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How are loans assessed under financial aid through the FSA?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Loans are monies that are advanced to students by way of a borrowing that pay for college or career school. These loans must be repaid along with any interest that accrues
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hello I need information about How to renew a registration
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You must renew your registration before it expires. Your new registration documents will arrive in the mail in approximately 10 days. Early renewal will not change the new expiration date of your new registration.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: but I have a situation, I don't know the correct amount that I should put in the check to renew, how do I do?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You can send a check payable to the 'Commissioner of Motor Vehicles', but do not write the amount
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I don't know the correct amount to put in the check to renew, how do I do it?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: As I said earlier, send a check made payable to the 'Commissioner of Motor Vehicles', but do not write the amount.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: and how do I renew if My registration is about to expire?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: It is very easy. You can renew online up to one year after the expiration date. You can download and print a temporary registration that is valid for 10 days on the day you renew.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: And how can I do if my address is not the same as when I gave the data?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Your email address on DMV files must be current. You cannot change it during the transaction. First change your address online and then renew.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I already submitted an application to the US Postal Service. UU?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: do not
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: and can I renew an online registration?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: if you can renew a registration online
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hey, hello, how is it going?. Em, its kinda shame but i heard about these services which support those people who pass through sexual traumas. How can anyone enjoy that?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you a veteran who experienced MST?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, not myself. I'm just asking.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: I guess it is, but, Are these services privacy protected? i mean, are they anonymous?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Of course they are. The well being of the veteran is a top priority, so you can get confidential counseling on any support you need.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: O.k, that good. Look, someone who pass through these could end on the streets, what could you do for him or her?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: They can contact the National Call center for Homeless veterans, 24/7. There, a trained VA counselor will offer information about homeless programs.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Umm, that must be very helpful. Say, lets imagine this person who wants to access the program doesn't have a VA care provider, it can still access the program?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Well, in that case, the person should go for its nearest VA medical center and ask to speak to an MST coordinator.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Um, makes sense. And for getting access to all this, do the individual need something like a disability rating or being in other VA services or having reported the trauma?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: No, it doesn't need to. He or she can access the program without any probation document.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I am looking to get information regarding a retirement benefits planner, where do I go to get this information?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you aware of the Government Pension Offset (GPO)?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, I have heard of this but would need more information on it
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Will you be in receipt of a pension for work that is not covered by Social Security?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, that does apply to me
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: In this case, any benefits that you may be able to receive on your spouse's record may be reduced. This reduction is referred to as GPO
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What information is required to calculate my benefits if I am affected by GPO?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: There are two things required; we will need to know your gross monthly pension from your job that is not covered by Social Security and then the estimated monthly amount of your Social Security benefit as a spouse, widow or widower, before the effect of GPO
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Where can I get further information regarding the calculation of my benefits upon retirement?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you eligible for widow's or widower's benefits, or have you no access to your spouse's estimate or records?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, I do not fit these criteria
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: hi
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: hi
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, i'm not scheduled for that, but wanted to know in any case.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: O.k. Another question, DMV doesn't provides any station signs, right?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: That's right. You can use sign shops and automotive supply stores to purchase those signs required for your station. Be aware that your signs requirements may vary depending on waht types of vehicles you will be inspecting.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Oh, i see. I heard that you don't do that, but i wanted to know it for sure. Now another topic. Does my station needs to be equipped with a computerized inspection system?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Not necessarily. The Computerized Vehicle Inspection System is just required in case you are going to inspect vehicles from the group 1a.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: If anyone wants to search this on your online platform, How they should look for it?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: The search should be under the "Inspection" topic.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: If i get approval after the inspector visit, What will happens next?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: If you have positive results, then you'll receive a license once you have purchased the computerized system if you are grup 1a  station.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can I use insurance documents from other states?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We can't accept any out-of-state vehicle insurance coverage of any kind.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: My vehicle was registered in New York. what insurance should I use?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: All vehicles registered in New York must have New York State auto liability insurance coverage.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I own a motorcycle, do I need to get insurance for it?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: If you're going to use your motorcycle on any public road or highway you must have insurance coverage. Motorcycle registrations are only valid for one year and expire on April 30.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I use my motorcycle only on the outskirts of the city?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Any public road counts so you have to get insurance coverage.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: With how many names can I register a vehicle?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: There're only 1 or 2 names allowed on a registration registrant. Both, the primary registrant and the co-registrant must sign the vehicle registration and provide proof of identity and date of birth.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hello, can you tell me about the maximum family amount please?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Of course. Each family member may be eligible for a monthly benefit of up to 50 percent of your disability benefit amount. However, there is a limit to the amount we can pay your family.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Is the sum of the benefits payable on your account greater than the family limit?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, it isn't.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: So, what about benefits if my spouse collects a higher Social Security benefit than me?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: In that case the spouse benefit amount will be permanently reduced by a percentage based on the number of months until his or her full retirement age.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Oh, so if she also works under Social Security that has an effect on the benefit as well?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, that is correct. His or her Social Security benefit on your record may be affected.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I see. And if she didn't work under Social Security that would not be an issue, right?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, that is correct.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: And when would benefits be payable for my spouse?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Benefits are payable for your spouse at age 62 or older.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hi. My son is a veteran who just returned home to us, and he seems to be having trouble with adjusting to life at home again. I don't really know what to do to help him but I want to help him.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We can absolutely help to get you the support that you and your son need. Did you have any particular questions or needs that you would like to address?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: It is your son who has veteran status, is that correct?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, it's my son.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You can take advantage of our Coaching Into Care program. You can call toll-free and talk to licensed psychologists and social workers to help you and your son on your journey.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: My son has attempted suicide in the past. My younger children know that something is different about him. But I don't know how to approach the topic with them without scaring them. Do you have any recommendations?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We have tips on how to approach that subject based on age level of who you are talking to. We even have age-appropriate tips for preschoolers and school-age children.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How would I talk to someone in the Coaching Into Care program?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: To reach a coach, you can call 888-823-7458, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Is there anywhere else I can go to read more information on my own?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, we also have the Coaching Into Care website that has information, tips, and resources for Veterans and their family members.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I have concerns that my son may attempt suicide again? Is there anyone I could talk to about that?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Your local VA medical center will have specially trained suicide prevention coordinators. And our Vet Centers can help you and your family help your son readjust to life at home again.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hey there. I was wondering which documents I need. I'm a bit confused.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: No problem. First of all, you will need a document proving the citizenship.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Have you NOT established your child's U.S. citizenship with us before?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, I haven't done that yet.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: In that case we need to see proof of U.S. citizenship. We can accept only certain documents as proof of U.S. citizenship. These include a U.S. birth certificate or U.S. passport.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I'm not a U.S. citizen?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Well, then Social Security will ask to see a current DHS document. A  I-551 Permanent Resident Card would be an acceptable document.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: And what if I don't have such a document available?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We may then accept the child's state issued non-drivers identification card or an adoption decree.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Oh, so an adoption decree would also work?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, that is correct.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Does Social Security require a proof of identity?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes. Your U.S. driver's license or a state-issued non-driver identification card or passport should be sufficient.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hello I need information on How do I get these disability rating benefits?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: To get these benefits, you must file a disability compensation claim. Find out how to file a disability compensation claim
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How much do you have a qualified injury of 30% or more that has worsened?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: do not
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: if a veteran has an immobilizing disability how is it rated?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You may be able to get a 100% temporary disability rating and disability compensation or benefits if you have this type of immobilizing disability.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can I get VA disability benefits?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text:
Yes, but if you had surgery, you must comply with the following:
     The surgery required a recovery time of at least one month or reports show that the surgery or treatment was for a service-related disability,
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if problems arose during surgery?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: If Surgery resulted in serious problems, such as: not being completely healed or Surgical wounds can benefit
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can I get VA disability benefits?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You may be able to get disability benefits if you had surgery or other treatment at a VA hospital, an approved hospital, or an outpatient facility for a military-related disability called service-related disability
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Hey there. I hope you can help me. I been having issues to fill my claim.

edus.text: And how much it will cost to use this service?

edus.text: In general, no individual or organization may charge you any fee, but this can change in unusual cases.

edus.text: What happens or what can I do if I get an very high fee?

edus.text: In that case you can challenge it

edus.text: To find an accredited representative or VSO, you can go to eBenefits to find a local representatives as recognized VSO, attorney and similar.

edus.text: I need information on how to get my driving record abstract.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: I can help you with that. Are you aware of what your driving record abstract entails?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I know a little about it, but I could definitely use more information.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Do you know how the abstract differs from your lifetime driving record?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, I do not.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Are you inquiring about a CDL driving record abstract?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, that's what I need.

edus.text: You can order your CDL driving record abstract online. You can also ask your employer to order them through the License Event Notification Service.

edus.text: Can I order by phone instead?

edus.text: Sorry, but no. These cannot be ordered by phone.

edus.text: No. You can not get your CDL or Lifetime abstract at a DMV office.

edus.text: What are Direct Plus Loans?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Direct Plus Loans are federal loans that parents of dependent undergraduate students can use to help pay for college or career school which help pay for education expenses not covered by financial aid.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Are you looking to apply?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No not at this time.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: When can I can my loan after it is disbursed?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You may cancel all or part of the loan within certain time frames and the information you receive along with your promissory note will explain the procedures and the time frames for cancelling your loans.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Is there a possibility that my loan can be forgiven or discharged?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Only under certain conditions can you have all or some of the loan discharged or forgiven.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Are you seeking Public Service Loan Forgiveness?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, I am seeking Public Service Loan Forgiveness.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You will need to repay your loans under an income-driven repayment plan then.

edus.text: II want to make some modifications to my truck and want to know if it will affect it's registration class.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, some modifications could affect your vehicle's registration class.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Have you already made modifications to your truck?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yeah, I'm in the process of modifying the exterior of my truck.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: In this case, you will need to get a vehicle weight slip. These can be obtained at the majority of vehicle and metal scrapyards.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How do I know if my modifications will change the class of my truck?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Your vehicle can remain in the passenger class if it meets certain criteria and unladen weight requirements.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Does your truck have an unladen weight of less than 6,001 lbs.?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: No, my truck weighs more than that.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Does this mean that I can't register it in the passenger class?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Although your truck weighs more than 6,000 lbs, you can still register it in the passenger class, if it's not being used exclusively for commercial purposes, does display any business ads, has a camper top that has one or more side windows completely enclosed, and has seats, seat fittings, and/or camping equipment installed in the bed.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Thanks for the information. What about cars? How can I register it from the passenger class to the commercial class?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Cars can be registered in the commercial class, if all seats are removed behind the driver and replaced with a permanent box or permanent rack to carry cargo.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Is there anywhere else I can obtain a weight slip for my truck?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes. Aside from vehicle and metal scrapyards, you can also receive a weight slip from some auto parts stores and mechanic shops, especially if they specialize in modifications.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How do I go about getting my car registered in the commercial class?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: After you have removed the seats behind you and replaced them with a permanent box or cargo rack, you should be able to register your car in the commercial class.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Can I get both a youth and PIRP discount?

edus.text: Ok; how can I reduce my points on my record then?

edus.text: If you find an accredited cours,e they will give you all the pertinent information.

edus.text: They'll tell you everything you need to know.

edus.text: Will my discount begin immediately according to NY law?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Immediately, retroactive to date of course completion.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Will taking the PIRP ensure I can't get a premium increase?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes and no. It does not cover overall or violation increases. It does provide a 10% reduction for three years for no fault and current liability.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: When will that take place?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: It can be issued as soon as the date of completion, potentially retroactively, depending on the insurer.

edus.text: Sounds good to me.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: For any other questions about premiums and discounts offered, please contact your insurer.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: How do I remove a corrective lens restriction by mail?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Do you want to remove the Corrective Lens restriction before it is time to renew your driver's license?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, that is what I want to do before I renew my driver license.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Okay, then you must get proof that you do not need corrective lenses and then order a replacement document and proof can be sbmitted to DMV electronically by a vision care provider enrolled in DMV Online Vision Registry.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Do I need to pass a vision test when I apply for a driver license or renew license?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Yes, you would have to pass a vision test for renewal or when apply for driver license and the test needs to show that you have a vision of at least 20/40 in either or both eyes with or without corrective lenses.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if the form has a print date of 3/04 or later?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: The vision care professional will mark a box on the form to indicate if the form is valid for 6 months or for 12 months after the date of the eye test.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if the Vision Test Report has a print date of 9/03 or earlier?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Then the form is valid for 6 months after the date of the eye test.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Do I have to take my vision test at the DMV office?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You can either take a vision test at the DMV office or prove that you meet our vision requirements by providing a Vision Test Report completed by a qualified eye care provider.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if my vision is impaired for night time?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Then you will have Daylight Driving Only restriction on your license.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I need to know what I can do to pay for course materials if I don't have any money.

edus.text: Well, that doesn't help me solve my issue. Is there any way to get course materials for less money?

edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Even then you may be able to save money by shopping around.

have you ever tried to apply

edus.text: What is the Blue Water Navy Vietnam Veterans Act?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Veterans who served on a Blue Water Navy vessel offshore of the Republic of Vietnam , or on another U.S. Navy or Coast Guard ship operating in the coastal waterways of Vietnam between January 9 , 1962 , and May 7 , 1975 , are now entitled to a presumption of service connection for illnesses related to Agent Orange exposure . This is a result of Public Law 116 - 23 , also known as the Blue Water Navy Vietnam Veterans Act of 2019 .

edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Contact the DMV Call Center. They will be able to assist you with any questions that you have about that topic.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I'm not able to surrender my plates after letting my insurance lapse?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: DMV has a procedure in place to exempt you from administrative actions once you return. You will need to provide a notarized statement that your vehicle was not used during the time in question, a copy of your military ID, and signed and dated copies of deployment and return papers. You will also need to provide proof of current insurance. If you cannot, you will need to surrender your plates.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Who issued these extensions for military personnel?

edus.text: NY Governor Andrew M. Cuomo issued these extensions under Executive Order No. 2.

edus.text: please, give me information about benefits planning for widows.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: there're around five million widows and widowers getting monthly Social Security benefits based on their deceased spouse earnings record.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: are the benefits really helpful?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: and, how soon could my widow receive start receiving the benefits?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: Particularly for aged women, the benefits help them keep out of poverty.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: In regards to your second question, it depends. Do you want to know about if she does not remarries?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: not really.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: and at any age?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: yes, tell me about that.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: or first tell me about the benefits at old ages.
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: ok. For your widow, the benefit could start as soon as she turns 60, getting reduced benefits, or full benefits at the full retirement age. If your widow is disabled she could start getting the benefits at age 50 if her disability started before your death or up to seven years after.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: and what if my widow qualifies for her own retirement benefits?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: in that case, she could switch to their own retirement benefits when turning 62 years old.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: and if she remarries before turning 60?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: if she's not disabled by the time she remarries then she would not qualify to receive the benefits while she's still married.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I adopted a foreign child, what do I need to get him a social security card?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: You need to provide the required documents, original or certified copies.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Do you need to claim your child now for tax purposes while waiting for the adoption process to end?
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: Yes, I do. What should I do?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: you should contact the Internal Revenue Service to ask them for Form W-7A, Application for Taxpayer Identification Number for Pending U.S. Adoptions.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: I'm guessing that notarized copies are not accepted? and can I use expired documents?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: That's correct, as mentioned previously, only original documents and certified copies are accepted. And no, expired documents are not accepted.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What should I use to prove my child's age?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: the child's foreign birth certificate is the preferred document.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: What if I can't get the birth certificate?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: then we'll ask for another suitable document, like a document given by the DHS that shows your child's age or the child's passport.
edus.speaker: agent

edus.text: and for me, what if I can't get the documents asked as proof of identity?
edus.speaker: user

edus.text: We'll ask you for another acceptable document, like your employee ID card.
edus.speaker: agent
